commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical blocker: improve executable-path redaction and add focused tests; no product decision is needed if the diagnostic remains detect-only.; Needs attention: The diff adds a user-copied Doctor diagnostic that can still disclose OS-home executable paths when OPENCLAW_HOME is set separately.; Review finding: [P2] Redact the OS home before printing execPath — src/commands/doctor-node-ru... (sha=7c2c383d6fe61a31b010cd28ca20946a8c62c372)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a detect-only `doctor:node-runtime` contribution that reports the current Node version, executable path, version-manager hint, and Node lifecycle advisories during `openclaw doctor`.

PR surface: Source +354, Tests +394. Total +748 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows `shortenHomePath` redacts the effective OpenClaw home, and tests confirm `OPENCLAW_HOME` takes precedence over `HOME`. A current-main bug repro is not applicable because the Node runtime note is new in this PR.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical blocker: improve executable-path redaction and add focused tests; no product decision is needed if the diagnostic remains detect-only.

target.title: feat(doctor): add Node.js runtime info health contribution
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new Doctor health contribution that displays Node.js runtime
diagnostics during `openclaw doctor`, providing essential context for
troubleshooting and proactive upgrade guidance.

## What it does

**Runtime summary note** (always shown):
Node 24.14.0 · ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node · via nvm

**Version manager detection** — identifies nvm, fnm, volta, asdf, n,
nodenv, nodebrew, and nvs from the executable path.

**Proactive warnings** (when applicable):
- Node version below minimum (>=22.19.0) — rarely triggers since the
  startup gate catches this first, but guards edge cases
- Node release in maintenance phase — shows months remaining until EOL
- Node release past end-of-life — recommends a current Active LTS release
- Older-than-recommended LTS — gentle nudge toward the recommended LTS

## Why

Doctor currently has no visibility into the Node.js runtime environment.
When users share Doctor output in issues or with maintainers, the Node
version and installation method are critical diagnostic context that was
previously missing.

This reuses existing infrastructure:
- `runtime-guard.ts` — `detectRuntime()`, `parseSemver()`, `runtimeSatisfies()`
- `runtime-paths.ts` — `isVersionManagedNodePath()`

No new dependencies. No runtime behavior changes outside of Doctor.

## Changes

New files:
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.ts` (~200 lines) — diagnostics
  collection, warning generation, summary formatting, contribution entry
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.test.ts` — 27 unit tests

Modified:
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts` — +1 import, +1 wrapper
  function, +1 contribution registration (after `bundled-plugin-runtime-deps`,
  before `state-integrity`)

## Test plan

- `pnpm check` — 0 errors ✅
- `vitest` — 27/27 tests ✅
- `pnpm build` — clean ✅

Test coverage:
- `detectVersionManagerName`: 11 tests (null, system, nvm, fnm, volta,
  asdf, n, nodenv, nodebrew, nvs, Windows backslash)
- `collectNodeRuntimeDiagnostics`: 3 tests (nvm path, unknown runtime,
  system install)
- `buildNodeRuntimeWarnings`: 8 tests (recommended version, below
  minimum, past EOL, maintenance phase, pre-maintenance nudge, current
  recommended, null major, future major)
- `buildNodeRuntimeSummary`: 5 tests (system install, named manager,
  unknown manager, null version, separator format)

---

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Doctor had no Node.js runtime summary or lifecycle warning. runtime-guard/runtime-paths expose detection helpers but no Doctor contribution surfaced them. This adds a `doctor:node-runtime` contribution that reports the running Node version, exec path, version-manager, and warns on below-minimum / EOL / maintenance runtimes.

- **Real environment tested:** Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Invoked the registered contribution entry point `noteNodeRuntime()` (the same function `runNodeRuntimeHealth` awaits in the Doctor health flow) so the output below is the actual `note()` render the Doctor command emits, plus injected lifecycle scenarios via the exported helpers.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran the registered Doctor contribution entry point via tsx against the real runtime:
```bash
$ node --import tsx run-node-runtime-note.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Actual rendered output. The first block is the real `note()` render from `noteNodeRuntime()` (home-redacted exec path); the rest are injected lifecycle scenarios computed against the real current date with the updated release schedule (Node 22/24/25/26):
```
Node.js runtime
  Node 24.14.0 · /usr/bin/node · system install
  (no advisory — supported)

Injected Node 25 (25.6.1, in maintenance, current date):
  Node 25 is in maintenance mode (EOL 2026-06-01, ~1 month remaining).
  Consider upgrading to a current Active LTS release for the latest features and longer support.

Injected Node 22 LTS (22.19.0, in maintenance):
  Node 22 LTS is in maintenance mode (EOL 2027-04-30, ~11 months remaining).
  Consider upgrading to Node 24 for the latest features and longer support.

Injected Node 20 (20.18.0, below the >=22.19.0 minimum):
  Node 20.18.0 does not meet the minimum requirement (>=22.19.0).
  Upgrade Node: https://nodejs.org/en/download
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real Node 24.14.0 runtime renders a clean Doctor note with no advisory (supported, system install). Injecting Node 25 — which is in maintenance with EOL 2026-06-01 — produces a maintenance advisory with ~1 month remaining and, correctly, does not suggest a downgrade to Node 24 (it recommends a current Active LTS release). Node 22 LTS produces its maintenance advisory; Node 20 (below the >=22.19.0 floor) produces a minimum-version warning. The exec path is home-redacted via the shared `shortenHomePath` helper.

- **What was not tested:** The full interactive `openclaw doctor` TUI run was not captured (it triggers a heavy dist build); instead the registered `noteNodeRuntime()` contribution entry point is invoked directly against the real runtime, producing the same `note()` render the Doctor flow emits. Lifecycle branches are exercised with injected versions because only one runtime is installed on the test host.
target.author: alkor2000

commands.author: samson910022

target.title: fix(status): warm model context-window cache before /status reads it
target.body:
## Problem
CLI \openclaw status\ shows \200K\ context window for models that haven't been discovered yet. The cache-warming logic (\ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded\) was never triggered during normal operation due to \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\ blockers in status paths.

## Root Cause
\ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded()\ had 4 gaps:
1. No startup warming — Gateway never fired it on boot
2. \prepareContextWindowCache\ short-circuits on \skipRuntimeConfigLoad=true\ (status path)
3. \status-text.ts\ explicitly disabled warming via \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\
4. \status.summary.ts\ (both call sites) also disabled warming

## Fix
- **Startup warming**: \server-startup-early.ts\ — fire-and-forget via \Promise.allSettled\
- **Path-based warming**: \status.summary.ts\, \status-text.ts\ — remove \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\
- **Config propagation**: \ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded(cfgOverride?)\ — optional parameter for caller-provided config
- **P2 cfg scoping**: \status.summary.runtime.ts\ — use warming wrapper
- **Singleton guard**: Prevent duplicate concurrent loads
- **Static imports**: Convert dynamic \import()\ to static imports in \context.ts\ to prevent \EnvironmentTeardownError\ on CI

## Files Changed (8 files, +172/-22)

### Cache-warming logic
- \src/agents/context.ts\ — Static imports, \ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded\ with \cfgOverride?\, startup warming, singleton guard
- \src/gateway/server-startup-early.ts\ — \Promise.allSettled\ startup warming
- \src/commands/status.summary.ts\ — Removed \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\, passes \sourceCfg\
- \src/commands/status.summary.runtime.ts\ — Warming wrapper import
- \src/status/status-text.ts\ — Removed \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\

### Tests (26 + 14 + 17 = 57 tests pass)
- \src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts\ — 26 regression tests covering all warming paths
- \src/commands/status.summary.test.ts\ — Updated 3 assertions for new warming behavior
- \src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts\ — Removed strict mock assertions (2 tests)

## Verification
- \context.lookup.test.ts\ — 26/26 ✅
- \status.summary.test.ts\ — 14/14 ✅
- \get-reply.fast-path.test.ts\ — 17/17 ✅
- Code-reviewer ✅ APPROVED
- Test-engineer ✅ APPROVED (zero regressions)

Closes #92760
